Add OAuth proxy endpoints to strip resource param for Entra compatibility

The MCP SDK (RFC 8707) sends resource=<server-origin> to the authorization
and token endpoints. Entra ID rejects this with AADSTS9010010 when the
resource doesn't match the scope audience (api://client-id).

Fix: Cloudflare Worker now proxies /oauth/authorize and /oauth/token,
stripping the resource parameter before forwarding to Entra. The
authorization_endpoint and token_endpoint in OAuth server metadata now
point to the Worker instead of Entra directly.

- /oauth/authorize: strips resource, 302 redirects to Entra auth endpoint
- /oauth/token: strips resource, proxies POST to Entra token endpoint
- Protected resource metadata resource field stays as origin (satisfies
  SDK's checkResourceAllowed validation)
- All three clients (VS Code, Claude Code, Open Code) now authenticate

Also update docs/11-readonly-mcp-setup.md architecture diagram and add
AADSTS9010010 troubleshooting section.

commit.message:
Add write operations gated by ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE (#12)

* Add write operations gated by ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE

Implement the full deposit write workflow, all behind an explicit opt-in:

  ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=true   (or '1') enables write tools
  ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=false  (default) — write tools not registered

## New tools (only registered when write is enabled)

Deposition CRUD:
  create_deposition   POST /api/deposit/depositions
  get_deposition      GET  /api/deposit/depositions/:id
  update_deposition   PUT  /api/deposit/depositions/:id  (metadata JSON)
  delete_deposition   DELETE /api/deposit/depositions/:id (drafts only)

File management:
  upload_file             PUT to bucket URL; accepts utf-8 or base64 content
  delete_deposition_file  DELETE /api/deposit/depositions/:id/files/:file_id

Deposition actions:
  publish_deposition   .../actions/publish
  edit_deposition      .../actions/edit  (unlock published record)
  discard_deposition   .../actions/discard
  new_version          .../actions/newversion

## Safety design

- Write tools are not registered at all when ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE is false,
  so AI agents never see them unless the operator has opted in.
- Each handler also checks the flag independently (defence-in-depth).
- All write methods call requireAuth() — clear error if key is missing.
- docker-compose.yml exposes ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE (default: false).

## Tests (test/zenodo-write.test.ts, 19 tests)

- Gate: write tools absent in tool list when disabled
- Gate: write tools present when ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=true
- Read-only tools still present when write is enabled
- 10 defence-in-depth handler tests (each write tool returns isError when flag off)
- 2 not-authenticated tests (write enabled, no API key)
- 4 live sandbox round-trip tests (create → upload → update → delete),
  activated only when ZENODO_SANDBOX_API_KEY is set

CI: passes ZENODO_SANDBOX_API_KEY secret and sets ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE
accordingly.

38/38 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review feedback: SSRF fix, type safety, encoding validation, upload size limit, description fix

commit.message:
feat: add playback controller hook and journal REST API endpoints

Add the time-lapse replay system for flywheel visualization (Enhancement 4/5):

- usePlaybackController hook: loads JSONL journals, replays events at
  variable speed via requestAnimationFrame, supports 7 speed tiers
  (0.5× to 100×), seek by position/iteration/act, step forward/backward,
  and speed-dependent batching (≥10× batch per frame, ≥25× act-transitions only)

- REST API endpoints in dashboard server:
  GET /api/runs — list recorded runs with journal metadata
  GET /api/runs/:id/journal — stream JSONL event journal
  GET /api/runs/:id/journal/index — return companion seek index

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Validate ZENODO_BASE_URL in ZenodoClient constructor (#8)

If ZENODO_BASE_URL is misconfigured (e.g. extra text appended through
a copy-paste error in the MCP config), the server previously threw an
opaque 'Invalid URL' error on every tool call that built a URL, while
get_auth_status silently succeeded. Now the constructor validates the
URL immediately at startup and emits a clear diagnostic message.

Added unit tests covering valid URLs, trailing-slash stripping, and
three invalid-URL cases (embedded whitespace, empty string, relative path).

feat: add Event Journal Writer for flywheel time-lapse recording

Implement append-only JSONL recorder that subscribes to the pipeline
PubSub event bus, enriches events with sequence numbers, iteration
tracking, and flywheel act classification, then periodically flushes
to disk with a companion index file for random-access replay.

Key components:
- JournaledEvent type with sequence, iteration, and act enrichment
- JournalIndex/JournalIterationIndex/JournalActIndex for seek support
- deriveAct pure function (server-side mirror of stageToAct)
- subscribeJournalWriter following the subscribeWsBroadcaster pattern
- Periodic flush with 10MB safety cap
- Companion .index.json written alongside the .jsonl

* test: add assembly coverage tests for LocationToTrsf and multi-shape loading

  exports the assembly structure; on reimport ImportLabel walks the
  component reference labels and calls LocationToTrsf for the translated
  component.  After MakeImprint the two daughter placements are verified
  to differ in X by exactly 50 mm.

* GetLogicalVolumesCompleteness — verifies GetLogicalVolumes() returns
  exactly one entry keyed by the part name for a single-shape STEP.

* fix(container): disable BUILD_TESTING in Dockerfile; drop yaml-cpp from spack

include(CTest) implicitly sets BUILD_TESTING=ON, which causes cmake to
require yaml-cpp even though no tests are being built in the container.
Add -DBUILD_TESTING=OFF to avoid this.

yaml-cpp is a test-only dependency and is not present in the EIC spack
buildcache (ghcr.io/eic/spack-v2025.11.0), so spack had to build it
from source — lengthening the base-layer build unnecessarily.  Remove
it from spack.yaml so the base image only contains runtime dependencies
(Geant4 and OpenCASCADE).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci(container): cache spack base layer in GHCR by spack.yaml hash

base-container.yml:
- Hash .github/spack.yaml (first 8 hex chars of SHA-256) to produce a
  stable tag spack-<hash> that changes only when the environment changes.
- After GHCR login, inspect ghcr.io/eic/g4occt:spack-<hash> to detect a
  cache hit; skip the 44-min spack install + buildcache push on hit.
- On cache miss, push the new image under the hash tag (not :base directly)
  so the immutable content-addressed entry survives future retagging.
- Always retag the hash-tagged image as :base via docker buildx imagetools
  create (manifest-only, no layer download) so downstream steps find it.
- Add docker/setup-buildx-action@v3 (needed for imagetools create).

container.yml:
- Add cache-from/cache-to (type=registry, :buildcache tag) to
  build-push-action so Docker BuildKit layer cache is stored in GHCR and
  reused across builds when only the G4OCCT source changes.

commit.message:
ci(clang-tidy): suppress per-file noise and exclude _fetchcontent (#325)

* ci(clang-tidy): suppress per-file noise and exclude _fetchcontent

Two fixes to the 'Run clang-tidy on all files' step (non-PR pushes):

1. Add -quiet to run-clang-tidy so the per-file verbose summary
   ('N warnings generated. Suppressed M warnings (N in non-user code
   ...). Use -header-filter=.* to display errors from all non-system
   headers.') is not printed for every analysed file.

2. Pass '^(?!.*_fetchcontent)' as the files-regex argument so that
   source files under _fetchcontent/ (googlebenchmark, googletest) are
   excluded from analysis.  compile_commands.json includes those TUs
   because they are built as part of the project, but they are not our
   code and should not be linted.

The 'Run clang-tidy on changed files' step (PR events) already has
-quiet and only analyses files that appear in the PR diff, so it is
unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci(clang-tidy): use -source-filter instead of positional regex

The positional 'files' argument to run-clang-tidy is a regex on the
file path (documented as 'Files to be processed (regex on path)' and
implemented with re.search()), so '^(?!.*_fetchcontent)' as a positional
arg is also correct.  However, the named -source-filter option makes the
intent unambiguous and is less likely to be misread as a literal filename.

-source-filter is applied with re.match() and achieves the same result:
files whose absolute path contains '_fetchcontent' are excluded from
analysis; all other project source files are analysed as before.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(coverage): move sanitizer/coverage options before target definitions (#323)

* fix(coverage): move sanitizer/coverage options before target definitions

CMake captures the directory COMPILE_OPTIONS into each target's property
at the point the target is created (add_library / add_executable).  The
sanitizer and coverage blocks were placed after all library and test
target definitions, so add_compile_options() / add_link_options() had no
effect on any of those targets.

Move all USE_ASAN, USE_UBSAN, USE_TSAN, and USE_COVERAGE blocks to
immediately after the compiler-warning options and before any target or
find_package() calls.  This ensures --coverage, -fsanitize=*, etc. are
applied to G4OCCT, G4OCCTFixtureSupport, all test executables, and the
standalone app.

Verified locally: after the fix the coverage report covers 1118/1366
lines (81.8%) and 112/116 functions (96.6%), up from the broken 4/7
(57.1%) that only reflected inline header methods.

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

* fix(tests): delete G4OCCTTestExceptionHandler in TearDown; suppress Geant4 vis leaks

The fix to CMakeLists.txt that moves sanitizer options before target
definitions now correctly instruments all test executables with ASAN/UBSAN.
Two classes of new LeakSanitizer failures are addressed here:

1. G4OCCTTestEnv::TearDown() leak (unit tests 1–92)
   SetUp() installs a heap-allocated G4OCCTTestExceptionHandler via
   G4StateManager::SetExceptionHandler(), which does not take ownership.
   TearDown() restored the previous handler but never deleted ours, leaking
   16 bytes per test run.  Fix: retrieve the current handler before restoring
   the previous one, then delete it.

2. Geant4 RayTracer / PhysicalVolumeModel / scene leaks (vis tests 138–180)
   Geant4's visualization infrastructure (G4RayTracerViewer, G4OutBitStream,
   G4PhysicalVolumeModel::CreateCurrentAttValues, G4Scene::AddWorldIfEmpty,
   G4VisCommandSceneAddVolume::SetNewValue, etc.) allocates objects that are
   intentionally not freed at process exit.  These are upstream Geant4 leaks,
   not G4OCCT bugs.  Add suppressions to .github/lsan.supp covering all
   stack frames seen in the CI run.

commit.message:
docs(coverage): link annotated HTML report from coverage summary page (#324)

- docs/index.html: extend the Docsify redirect plugin to also redirect
  #/coverage/... hash paths to the real HTML files, matching the existing
  behaviour for #/api/... paths.  Without this the browser tries to fetch
  coverage/index.html as Markdown and renders nothing.

- generate_coverage_report.py: add a 'View annotated HTML coverage report'
  link immediately after the summary table, pointing to ../coverage/index.html
  (relative to reports/coverage.md).  Also strip the CI absolute path prefix
  (stored under 'root' in the gcovr JSON summary) from per-file filenames so
  the table shows readable relative paths like 'src/G4OCCTSolid.cc' instead
  of the full /home/runner/... path.
